title: Wall Push-Ups
instructions: Stand facing a wall.
Place palms flat at shoulder height.
Lower chest toward wall slowly.
Push back to start.
whyBullets: Activates upper body muscles safely.
Boosts blood flow and energy.
Improves confidence through movement.
safetyNotes: Stop if you feel shoulder pain.

title: Chair Sit-to-Stand
instructions: Sit tall on a chair with feet flat.
Stand up without using hands if possible.
Lower back down slowly.
Repeat steadily.
formTips: Keep knees in line with toes.
whyBullets: Strengthens legs for everyday function.
Improves circulation after long sitting.
Builds lower-body endurance.
safetyNotes: Use hands if balance feels unstable.

title: Standing Shoulder Rolls
instructions: Stand tall or sit upright.
Roll shoulders up, back and down slowly.
Reverse direction after 30 seconds.
whyBullets: Reduces neck and shoulder tension.
Improves posture awareness.
Helps reset stress response.

title: Bodyweight Squats
instructions: Stand with feet shoulder-width apart.
Push hips back and bend knees.
Lower until thighs are comfortable.
Push through heels to stand.
whyBullets: Builds full lower-body strength.
Boosts endorphins through compound movement.
Improves stability and mobility.

title: Wall Calf Raises
instructions: Stand upright holding wall for balance.
Raise heels off the floor.
Lower slowly and repeat.
whyBullets: Improves circulation in lower legs.
Helps reduce stiffness from sitting.
Supports ankle stability.

title: Plank Hold
instructions: Start in forearm plank position.
Keep body in straight line.
Hold for 20-40 seconds.
Rest and repeat.
whyBullets: Strengthens deep core stabilisers.
Improves posture and spinal support.
Builds mental resilience.
safetyNotes: Stop if lower back pain occurs.

title: Lunges
instructions: Step forward with one leg.
Lower back knee toward floor.
Push back to standing.
Alternate legs.
whyBullets: Builds balance and coordination.
Strengthens single-leg control.
Improves athletic movement.

title: Incline Push-Ups (Counter)
instructions: Place hands on a stable counter or desk edge.
Step feet back into a straight line.
Lower chest toward the edge under control.
Press back up and repeat.
formTips: Brace your core so hips don't sag.
whyBullets: Builds pushing strength with a lower load than floor push-ups.
Improves upper-body endurance for posture and daily movement.
Quick sets create a confidence and energy boost.
safetyNotes: Use a stable surface that won't slip.
Stop if you feel wrist pain; adjust hand position.

title: Knee Push-Ups
instructions: Start in a plank position then drop knees to the floor.
Keep a straight line from shoulders to knees.
Lower chest toward the floor slowly.
Press back up and repeat.
whyBullets: Strengthens chest and arms with reduced intensity.
Improves control and stability through the shoulders.
Helps build momentum toward full push-ups.
safetyNotes: Place a towel under knees if needed.

title: Glute Bridge
instructions: Lie on your back with knees bent and feet flat.
Brace core and squeeze glutes.
Lift hips until body forms a straight line shoulders-to-knees.
Lower slowly and repeat.
whyBullets: Activates glutes to counter long periods of sitting.
Supports lower-back comfort through better hip strength.
Improves circulation and posture control.
safetyNotes: Stop if you feel sharp lower-back pain.

title: Dead Bug
instructions: Lie on your back with arms up and knees bent at 90 degrees.
Slowly extend one leg and the opposite arm toward the floor.
Return to start and switch sides.
Repeat with slow control.
formTips: Keep lower back gently pressed to the floor.
whyBullets: Builds deep core stability for posture and back support.
Improves coordination and movement control.
Low-impact core work can reduce stress tension.
safetyNotes: Stop if your back arches and you can't control it.
Reduce range of motion if needed.

title: Bird Dog
instructions: Start on hands and knees.
Extend one arm forward and the opposite leg back.
Hold briefly, then return to start.
Switch sides and repeat.
formTips: Keep hips square to the floor.
whyBullets: Improves core stability and spinal control.
Builds balance and coordination.
Helps reduce stiffness from long sitting sessions.
safetyNotes: Stop if wrist pain occurs; try fists or forearms.

title: Standing March (High Knees Light)
instructions: Stand tall with feet hip-width apart.
Lift one knee to a comfortable height.
Lower and switch legs rhythmically.
Continue at a steady pace.
formTips: Keep chest up and breathe steadily.
whyBullets: Raises heart rate quickly without equipment.
Improves hip mobility and circulation.
Short cardio bursts can boost alertness and mood.
safetyNotes: Hold a wall or chair if balance is shaky.
Keep steps soft to protect joints.

title: Wall Slide (Shoulder Mobility)
instructions: Stand with back against a wall, elbows bent like goalposts.
Keep wrists and elbows near the wall.
Slowly slide arms up as high as comfortable.
Slide back down and repeat.
formTips: Keep ribs down; don't flare chest.
whyBullets: Improves shoulder mobility and posture control.
Activates upper-back muscles that support healthy sitting.
Helps relieve tension from gaming posture.

title: Hip Hinge (Good Morning)
instructions: Stand with feet hip-width apart, knees soft.
Push hips back while keeping back flat.
Lower torso until you feel a stretch in hamstrings.
Stand tall by squeezing glutes.
formTips: Hinge at hips, not the lower back.
whyBullets: Strengthens posterior chain to counter sitting posture.
Improves hip mechanics and back comfort.
Builds body awareness and movement confidence.
safetyNotes: Move slowly and stop if pain appears.

title: Side Plank (Knee)
instructions: Lie on your side and prop up on your forearm.
Bend knees and lift hips off the floor.
Hold 15-30 seconds.
Switch sides and repeat.
formTips: Lift from the hips, not the neck.
whyBullets: Strengthens side core for better posture.
Improves stability and spinal support.
Builds resilience with a simple hold.

title: Reverse Lunge (Step Back)
instructions: Stand tall with feet hip-width apart.
Step one leg back and lower into a lunge.
Push through front heel to return to start.
Alternate legs.
formTips: Front knee stays over the mid-foot.
whyBullets: Strengthens legs and glutes with less knee stress than forward lunges.
Improves balance and coordination.
Boosts lower-body power and stability.
safetyNotes: Hold a wall if balance is unstable.
Avoid banging the back knee on the floor.

title: Step-Ups (Stairs)
instructions: Use a stable step or bottom stair.
Step up with one foot and stand tall.
Step down under control.
Alternate legs.
whyBullets: Builds practical leg strength and stability.
Improves circulation and endurance quickly.
Supports better posture and hip strength.

title: Wall Sit
instructions: Lean back against a wall and slide down.
Stop when knees are at a comfortable bend.
Hold 20-45 seconds.
Stand up, rest, and repeat.
formTips: Keep back flat on the wall.
whyBullets: Builds leg endurance with minimal movement.
Creates a strong burn that boosts endorphins.
Improves mental toughness through controlled holds.
safetyNotes: Don't force knees past comfort range.

title: Mountain Climbers (Slow)
instructions: Start in a high plank position.
Bring one knee toward chest slowly.
Switch legs under control.
Keep rhythm steady.
whyBullets: Combines core stability with light cardio.
Boosts circulation and alertness fast.
Improves shoulder and hip control.
safetyNotes: Stop if wrists hurt; try fists or incline.

title: Glute Bridge March
instructions: Start in a glute bridge with hips lifted.
Lift one foot a few inches while keeping hips level.
Place it down and switch sides.
Continue alternating slowly.
whyBullets: Strengthens glutes and core together.
Improves pelvic stability and posture.
Helps counter hip tightness from sitting.
safetyNotes: Stop if lower back takes over.

title: Standing Hip Circles
instructions: Stand with feet hip-width apart.
Place hands on hips.
Circle hips slowly in one direction for 30 seconds.
Reverse direction for 30 seconds.
whyBullets: Improves hip mobility and reduces stiffness.
Encourages blood flow after long sitting.
Quick mobility can ease tension and stress.
safetyNotes: Stop if you feel sharp pain.

title: Ankle Pumps (Seated)
instructions: Sit upright with feet flat.
Lift toes up, then press toes down.
Repeat steadily for 60-90 seconds.
Rest briefly and repeat.
whyBullets: Boosts lower-leg circulation during long sessions.
Helps reduce stiffness and restlessness.
Low-effort movement supports focus and comfort.

title: Neck Chin Tucks
instructions: Sit or stand tall.
Gently tuck chin straight back (make a double-chin).
Hold 2 seconds, then relax.
Repeat slowly.
whyBullets: Strengthens neck posture muscles for screen time.
Can reduce tension headaches linked to forward head posture.
Small corrections improve comfort and focus.

title: Scapular Retractions (Wall)
instructions: Stand with back against a wall.
Place elbows and forearms against the wall if possible.
Squeeze shoulder blades down and back.
Relax and repeat.
whyBullets: Activates upper-back muscles that support posture.
Helps reverse rounded-shoulder gaming posture.
Improves shoulder comfort and stability.

title: Towel Row (Isometric)
instructions: Hold a towel with both hands in front of you.
Pull hands apart to create tension in the towel.
Hold 10-20 seconds while squeezing upper back.
Relax and repeat for several rounds.
formTips: Keep shoulders down away from ears.
whyBullets: Strengthens pulling muscles without weights.
Balances pushing work and improves posture.
Isometrics build control and body awareness.
safetyNotes: Stop if elbow or shoulder pain occurs.

title: Superman Hold (Short)
instructions: Lie face down with arms forward.
Lift chest and legs slightly off the floor.
Hold 5-10 seconds.
Lower and repeat.
formTips: Lift small; control is the goal.
whyBullets: Strengthens back-body muscles for posture support.
Counters slumped sitting and improves spinal endurance.
Short holds build confidence and control.

title: Hamstring Stretch (Doorway)
instructions: Lie near a doorway or wall.
Place one leg up against the wall/doorframe.
Keep the other leg relaxed on the floor.
Hold 30-45 seconds and switch sides.
whyBullets: Reduces hamstring tightness from long sitting.
Improves comfort for hips and lower back.
Calm stretching can downshift stress.
safetyNotes: Don't force the stretch; keep it gentle.
Stop if you feel nerve-like pain.

title: Couch Stretch (Hip Flexor)
instructions: Kneel with one knee near a couch or chair.
Place the back foot on the couch edge if comfortable.
Keep torso tall and gently squeeze glute of the back leg.
Hold 20-40 seconds and switch sides.
formTips: Keep ribs down; don't arch lower back.
Squeeze glute for a better stretch.
whyBullets: Opens hip flexors tightened by long sitting.
Can reduce anterior hip and low-back tension.
Improves stride mechanics and posture.

title: Bear Hover (Knees Under Hips)
instructions: Start on hands and knees.
Tuck toes and lift knees 1-2 inches off the floor.
Hold 10-20 seconds.
Lower, rest, and repeat.
formTips: Keep back flat and core braced.
Press the floor away with hands.
whyBullets: Builds full-body tension and core stability.
Strengthens shoulders and posture muscles.
Short holds develop resilience and focus.
safetyNotes: Stop if wrist pain occurs; try fists.

title: Jumping Jacks (Low Impact)
instructions: Stand tall with arms at sides.
Step one foot out while raising arms overhead.
Step back to center while lowering arms.
Alternate sides at a steady rhythm.
whyBullets: Raises heart rate without heavy joint impact.
Improves circulation and alertness fast.
Movement bursts can reduce anxious restlessness.

title: Shadow Boxing (30-60s Rounds)
instructions: Stand in a relaxed stance with hands up.
Throw light jabs and crosses into the air.
Add gentle slips or steps if comfortable.
Work in short rounds with brief rests.
formTips: Stay relaxed; speed comes from looseness.
whyBullets: Boosts heart rate and energy quickly.
Improves coordination and stress release.
Creates a strong endorphin lift with minimal setup.

title: Doorway Chest Stretch
instructions: Stand in a doorway with forearm on the frame.
Step forward slowly until you feel a chest stretch.
Hold 20-40 seconds.
Switch sides and repeat.
formTips: Keep shoulder down away from ear.
whyBullets: Opens chest to counter rounded shoulders.
Improves posture and shoulder comfort.
Gentle stretching can reduce stress tension.
safetyNotes: Don't force the stretch; keep it mild.

title: Thoracic Twist (Seated)
instructions: Sit tall with feet flat.
Place one hand on opposite knee.
Gently rotate torso to one side.
Hold briefly, return, and switch sides.
formTips: Rotate from upper back, not hips.
whyBullets: Improves upper-back mobility for posture.
Helps reduce stiffness from desk/gaming time.
Small mobility breaks improve comfort and focus.
safetyNotes: Stop if sharp back pain occurs.

title: Reverse Snow Angels (Floor)
instructions: Lie face down with forehead supported on hands or towel.
Lift arms slightly off the floor.
Sweep arms in a wide arc from hips toward overhead.
Return and repeat slowly.
whyBullets: Strengthens upper-back muscles for better posture.
Improves shoulder control and stability.
Counters rounded shoulders from screen time.
safetyNotes: Keep movement small if shoulders are tight.

title: Tricep Dips (Chair, Assisted)
instructions: Place hands on chair edge behind you.
Slide hips forward with knees bent.
Lower slightly by bending elbows.
Press back up and repeat.
whyBullets: Builds tricep strength with minimal equipment.
Improves pushing endurance and upper-body confidence.
Short strength sets boost energy and mood.

title: Wall Hand Push (Isometric)
instructions: Stand facing a wall with palms on the wall.
Push into the wall hard without moving.
Hold 10-20 seconds.
Relax and repeat.
whyBullets: Builds strength and muscle activation without joint strain.
Isometrics create a quick confidence boost.
Great low-energy option to build momentum.
safetyNotes: Stop if you feel shoulder discomfort.

title: Side Steps (Mini Squat)
instructions: Stand with feet hip-width apart and soften knees.
Sit back slightly into a mini squat.
Step to the side, then bring feet back under hips.
Repeat both directions.
whyBullets: Strengthens glute stabilisers for hip and knee support.
Improves lateral movement and balance.
Good for posture and reducing sitting stiffness.
safetyNotes: Avoid knee pain; reduce depth if needed.

title: Single-Leg Balance (30s)
instructions: Stand near a wall for support.
Lift one foot slightly off the floor.
Hold 20-30 seconds.
Switch sides and repeat.
formTips: Fix eyes on a point ahead.
whyBullets: Improves balance and ankle stability.
Supports better movement control and injury prevention.
Simple focus work can calm the nervous system.

title: Single-Leg Calf Raises
instructions: Stand near a wall for balance.
Lift one foot off the floor.
Rise onto toes of standing foot.
Lower slowly and repeat, then switch legs.
whyBullets: Builds calf strength and ankle stability.
Improves circulation and lower-leg endurance.
Supports comfort during long sitting sessions.
safetyNotes: Stop if you feel sharp Achilles pain.

title: Hollow Hold (Tucked)
instructions: Lie on your back with knees tucked toward chest.
Lift shoulders slightly off the floor.
Keep lower back gently pressed down.
Hold 10-20 seconds and repeat.
formTips: Keep breathing; don't brace with your neck.
Hold a smaller position if needed.
whyBullets: Builds deep core tension for strong posture.
Improves control and stability under pressure.
Short challenging holds build mental toughness.
safetyNotes: Stop if lower back pain occurs.

title: Push-Up (Full)
instructions: Start in a high plank with hands under shoulders.
Lower chest toward the floor with control.
Keep body in a straight line.
Press back up and repeat.
whyBullets: Builds full upper-body pushing strength.
Improves shoulder stability and core tension.
Strong compound effort boosts endorphins and confidence.
safetyNotes: Stop if wrist or shoulder pain occurs.
Use incline or knees if form breaks.

title: Squat to Calf Raise
instructions: Perform a controlled bodyweight squat.
Stand up and rise onto toes at the top.
Lower heels and repeat.
formTips: Keep chest lifted during the squat.
Move smoothly between squat and calf raise.
whyBullets: Combines strength and circulation in one movement.
Improves leg endurance and blood flow.
Great quick-hit exercise for energy and focus.
safetyNotes: Hold a wall if balance is unstable.

title: Split Squat (Static Lunge)
instructions: Stand in a split stance (one foot forward, one back).
Lower straight down under control.
Press through front heel to rise.
Repeat then switch stance.
whyBullets: Builds strong single-leg strength and stability.
Improves balance and athletic movement control.
Challenging work boosts endorphins and confidence.
safetyNotes: Reduce range if knee discomfort appears.
Hold a wall for stability if needed.

title: Backpack Romanian Deadlift
instructions: Hold a backpack close to your body.
Soften knees and hinge hips back with a flat back.
Lower until you feel hamstrings stretch.
Stand tall by squeezing glutes.
formTips: Keep weight close to your body.
Hinge at hips; don't round spine.
whyBullets: Builds posterior-chain strength for posture and back support.
Improves hip mechanics and stability.
Strength work increases confidence and resilience.
safetyNotes: Use light weight and perfect form.

title: Backpack Overhead Press
instructions: Hold a backpack at shoulder height.
Brace core and press overhead.
Lower under control.
Repeat steadily.
whyBullets: Builds shoulder strength with simple equipment.
Improves upper-body stability and posture.
Strength progress boosts confidence and motivation.
safetyNotes: Use a light backpack weight first.

title: Backpack Bent-Over Row
instructions: Hold a backpack by the top handle(s).
Hinge hips back with a flat back.
Row the backpack toward your ribs.
Lower slowly and repeat.
whyBullets: Strengthens pulling muscles to balance posture.
Supports shoulder health and stability.
Improves upper-back endurance for long sitting.
safetyNotes: Use manageable weight to protect form.

title: Burpees (No Push-Up)
instructions: Stand tall, then squat and place hands on the floor.
Step feet back into a plank (no push-up).
Step feet forward to squat position.
Stand up and add a small jump if comfortable.
formTips: Move smoothly; don't rush sloppy reps.
whyBullets: Full-body movement boosts heart rate quickly.
Strong endorphin hit improves mood and energy.
Builds resilience and conditioning with no gear.
safetyNotes: Step instead of jumping if joints are sensitive.
Stop if dizziness or sharp pain occurs.

title: Plank Shoulder Taps (Slow)
instructions: Start in a high plank.
Tap one shoulder with opposite hand.
Place hand back down and switch sides.
Keep hips steady and move slowly.
formTips: Keep hips as still as possible.
whyBullets: Builds anti-rotation core strength for posture.
Improves shoulder stability and control.
Develops focus and calm through slow control.
safetyNotes: Stop if wrist pain occurs; try incline.
Keep reps slow to protect form.

title: Sprint in Place (20s Rounds)
instructions: Stand tall and begin running in place.
Pump arms and keep steps light.
Work for 15-20 seconds.
Rest 20-40 seconds and repeat.
whyBullets: Rapid cardio burst boosts alertness fast.
Improves circulation after long sitting sessions.
Creates a strong endorphin lift and momentum.
safetyNotes: Reduce intensity if you feel lightheaded.
Avoid if you have acute joint pain.
